content.full_text:
April 1, 1992


Diane M. Fishburn
Olson, Connelly, Hagel,
Fong, & Leidigh
300 Capitol Mall Ste. 350
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Your Request for Confirmation
of Telephone Advice
Our File No. I-92-162

Dear Ms. Fishburn:

You have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided
to you concerning the campaign disclosure provisions of the
Political Reform Act.¹ Because you have not identified the
officeholder on whose behalf you are requesting this advice we are
treating your request as one for informal assistance pursuant to
Regulation 18329(c)(3).²

Your letter accurately summarizes my advice that a transfer
of funds between committees controlled by a state senator, who is
not up for election in 1992, would not be considered a
contribution within the meaning of Section 84200.5. Therefore, a
first preeelection statement would not be required, so long as none
of the senator's controlled committees made contributions or


___________________________
¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to.the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

² Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or written advice. (Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sections
18329(c)(3).)


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • .(916) 322-56(

Page 2
Fishburn, Diane


independent expenditures to support or oppose other candidates or
committees within the first preelection period.

     If you have additional questions, please contact me at (916)
322-5662.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Scott Hallabrin
                                        Acting General Counsel
                                        [signature]
                                        By: Wayne P. Imberi
                                            Political Reform Consultant

sections.conclusion: Your letter accurately summarizes my advice that a transfer of funds between committees controlled by a state senator, who is not up for election in 1992, would not be considered a contribution within the meaning of Section 84200.5. Therefore, a first preelection statement would not be required, so long as none of the senator's controlled committees made contributions or independent expenditures to support or oppose other candidates or committees within the first preelection period.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a transfer of funds between committees controlled by a state senator not up for election in 1992 constitutes a contribution requiring a first preelection statement under the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Fund transfers between committees controlled by a state senator not up for election in 1992 do not constitute contributions under Section 84200.5, and thus do not require a first preelection statement, provided the committees made no contributions or independent expenditures to other candidates or committees during the preelection period.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is confirmation of telephone advice (informal assistance). No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION section headers present; extracted text from letter body. Treated as correspondence rather than formal advice letter due to informal nature and lack of immunity provision. Document references Regulation 18329(c)(3) for informal assistance status.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a transfer of funds between committees controlled by a state senator not up for election in 1992 constitutes a contribution requiring a first preelection statement under the Political Reform Act.

CONCLUSION: Your letter accurately summarizes my advice that a transfer of funds between committees controlled by a state senator, who is not up for election in 1992, would not be considered a contribution within the meaning of Section 84200.5. Therefore, a first preelection statement would not be required, so long as none of the senator's controlled committees made contributions or independent expenditures to support or oppose other candidates or committees within the first preelection period.

embedding.summary: FPPC responds to a request for confirmation of telephone advice regarding campaign disclosure provisions under the Political Reform Act, specifically whether fund transfers between a state senator's controlled committees trigger contribution reporting requirements.
